title: Leria rotundifolia
taxonomy: Leria rotundifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to Madagascar, and its classification is part of the larger Apocynaceae order within the angiosperm group. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes coffee and gardenias. The classification of Leria rotundifolia reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this family. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Leria rotundifolia, also known as Round-leaved Leria, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant's leaves and roots are commonly used in herbal remedies due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Some studies suggest that extracts from Leria rotundifolia may have antimicrobial and antifungal effects, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of certain infections. Additionally, the plant is believed to have wound-healing properties and may be used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Leria rotundifolia for medicinal use.

The plant's leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. side_effects: Leria rotundifolia is a plant species that may cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its sap or extracts.
